response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: This meta-analysis contradicts decades of peer-reviewed research consensus (including Pashler et al. 2008, Cormier et al. 2012) showing learning-styles matching produces no achievement benefits, and the effect sizes reported (d=0.62) are implausibly large given the robust null findings in actual published literature on this topic.
